name: MSBuild
help: The Microsoft Build Engine is a platform for building applications. This engine, which is also known as MSBuild, provides an XML schema for a project file that controls how the build platform processes and builds software. Visual Studio uses MSBuild, but it doesn't depend on Visual Studio. By invoking msbuild.exe on your project or solution file, you can orchestrate and build products in environments where Visual Studio isn't installed. Visual Studio uses MSBuild to load and build managed projects. The project files in Visual Studio (.csproj,.vbproj, vcxproj, and others) contain MSBuild XML code that executes when you build a project by using the IDE. Visual Studio projects import all the necessary settings and build processes to do typical development work, but you can extend or modify them from within Visual Studio or by using an XML editor.

properties.name: TargetPath
properties.help: The solution or project file on which MSBuild is executed.

properties.name: DetailedSummary
properties.help: Show detailed information at the end of the build log about the configurations that were built and how they were scheduled to nodes.

properties.name: MaxCpuCount
properties.help:
Specifies the maximum number of concurrent processes to use when building. If you don't include this switch, the default value is 1. If you include this switch without specifying a value, MSBuild will use up to the number of processors in the computer. For more information, see Building Multiple Projects in Parallel.

The following example instructs MSBuild to build using three MSBuild processes, which allows three projects to build at the same time:

msbuild myproject.proj /maxcpucount:3

properties.name: NodeReuse
properties.help:
Enable or disable the re-use of MSBuild nodes. You can specify the following values:

  • true: Nodes remain after the build finishes so that subsequent builds can use them (default).
  • false. Nodes don't remain after the build completes.

A node corresponds to a project that's executing. If you include the /maxcpucount switch, multiple nodes can execute concurrently.

properties.name: NoLogo
properties.help: Don't display the startup banner or the copyright message.

properties.name: TargetPlatform
properties.help: The target platform for which the project is built to run on.

properties.name: Properties
properties.help:
Set or override the specified project-level properties, where name is the property name and value is the property value. Specify each property separately, or use a semicolon or comma to separate multiple properties, as the following example shows:

/property:WarningLevel=2;OutDir=bin\Debug

delegates.name: OutDir
delegates.help: Specifies the output directory for the project.

delegates.name: RunCodeAnalysis
delegates.help: Enables or disables code analysis.

delegates.name: NoWarn
delegates.help: Suppresses the specified warnings.

delegates.name: WarningsAsErrors
delegates.help: Treats the specified warnings as errors.

delegates.name: WarningLevel
delegates.help: Specifies the warning level (0-4).

delegates.name: Configuration
delegates.help: Specifies the build configuration (e.g., Debug or Release).

delegates.name: TreatWarningsAsErrors
delegates.help: Treats all warnings as errors.

delegates.name: AssemblyVersion
delegates.help: Specifies the version of the assembly.

delegates.name: FileVersion
delegates.help: Specifies the version of the file.

delegates.name: InformationalVersion
delegates.help: Specifies the informational version of the assembly.

delegates.name: PackageOutputPath
delegates.help: Specifies the output path for the NuGet package.

delegates.name: IncludeSymbols
delegates.help: Specifies whether to include symbols in the package.

delegates.name: PackageId
delegates.help: Specifies the ID of the NuGet package.

delegates.name: PackageVersion
delegates.help: Specifies the version of the NuGet package.

delegates.name: PackageVersionPrefix
delegates.help: Specifies the prefix for the NuGet package version.

delegates.name: PackageVersionSuffix
delegates.help: Specifies the suffix for the NuGet package version.

delegates.name: Authors
delegates.help: Specifies the authors of the NuGet package.

delegates.name: Title
delegates.help: Specifies the title of the NuGet package.

delegates.name: Description
delegates.help: Specifies the description of the NuGet package.

delegates.name: Copyright
delegates.help: Specifies the copyright notice for the NuGet package.

delegates.name: PackageRequireLicenseAcceptance
delegates.help: Specifies whether the package requires license acceptance.

delegates.name: PackageLicenseUrl
delegates.help: Specifies the URL for the package's license.

delegates.name: PackageProjectUrl
delegates.help: Specifies the URL for the package's project page.

delegates.name: PackageIconUrl
delegates.help: Specifies the URL for the package's icon.

delegates.name: PackageTags
delegates.help: Specifies the tags for the NuGet package.

delegates.name: PackageReleaseNotes
delegates.help: Specifies the release notes for the NuGet package.

delegates.name: RepositoryUrl
delegates.help: Specifies the URL for the package's repository.

delegates.name: RepositoryType
delegates.help: Specifies the type of the package's repository.

delegates.name: RestoreSources
delegates.help: List of package sources.

delegates.name: RestorePackagesPath
delegates.help: User packages folder path.

delegates.name: RestoreDisableParallel
delegates.help: Limit downloads to one at a time.

delegates.name: RestoreConfigFile
delegates.help: Path to a Nuget.Config file to apply.

delegates.name: RestoreNoCache
delegates.help: If true, avoids using the web cache.

delegates.name: RestoreIgnoreFailedSources
delegates.help: If true, ignores failing or missing package sources.

delegates.name: RestoreTaskAssemblyFile
delegates.help: Path to <c>NuGet.Build.Tasks.dll</c>.

delegates.name: RestoreGraphProjectInput
delegates.help: Semicolon-delimited list of projects to restore, which should contain absolute paths.

delegates.name: RestoreOutputPath
delegates.help: Output folder, defaulting to the obj folder.

delegates.name: SymbolPackageFormat
delegates.help: Format for packaging symbols.

delegates.name: PublishProfile
delegates.help: Name of ClickOnce publication .pubxml file found in project properties folder.

delegates.name: PublishDir
delegates.help: Path of publication directory.

delegates.name: RuntimeIdentifiers
delegates.help: RuntimeIdentifiers of RFID see .NET RID Catalog

delegates.name: UpdateUrl
delegates.help: Optional. Set its value if different from InstallURL.

delegates.name: BootstrapperEnabled
delegates.help: Determines whether to generate the setup.exe bootstrapper. Build ClickOnce applications from the command line

delegates.name: GenerateManifests
delegates.help: GenerateManifests and SignManifests must be true to work.

delegates.name: Platform
delegates.help: Output assembly platform C# Compiler Options that control compiler output

delegates.name: TargetFramework
delegates.help: Set TargetFramework of output assembly. Target frameworks in SDK-style projects

delegates.name: ApplicationVersion
delegates.help: Specifies the application version for ClickOnce deployment.

delegates.name: ApplicationRevision
delegates.help: Specifies the application revision for ClickOnce deployment.

delegates.name: CreateWebPageOnPublish
delegates.help: Specifies whether to create a web page when publishing.

delegates.name: Install
delegates.help: Determines whether the application is an installed application or a run-from-Web application Build .NET ClickOnce applications from the command line

delegates.name: InstallFrom
delegates.help: Possible installation source InstallFrom Enum

delegates.name: MapFileExtensions
delegates.help: Optional. Defaults to false. If true, all files in the deployment must have a ".deploy" extension. DeployManifest.MapFileExtensions Property

delegates.name: OpenBrowserOnPublish
delegates.help: OpenBrowserOnPublish

delegates.name: PublishProtocol
delegates.help: Default is "ClickOnce"

delegates.name: PublishReadyToRun
delegates.help: Compiles application assemblies as ReadyToRun (R2R) format. R2R is a form of ahead-of-time (AOT) compilation.

delegates.name: PublishSingleFile
delegates.help: Packages the app into a platform-specific single-file executable.

delegates.name: SelfContained
delegates.help: Specifies whether to publish the .NET runtime with your application so the runtime doesn't need to be installed on the target machine.

delegates.name: SignManifests
delegates.help: Specifies whether to sign the ClickOnce manifests.

delegates.name: SkipPublishVerification
delegates.help: Specifies whether to skip verification after publishing.

delegates.name: UpdateEnabled
delegates.help: Specifies whether ClickOnce updates are enabled.

delegates.name: ManifestKeyFile
delegates.help: Specifies the key file for signing the manifests.

delegates.name: CreateDesktopShortcut
delegates.help: Specifies whether to create a desktop shortcut for the ClickOnce application.

delegates.name: UpdateRequired
delegates.help: Specifies whether a ClickOnce update is required.

properties.name: Restore
properties.help: Runs the <c>Restore</c> target prior to building the actual targets.

properties.name: Targets
properties.help:
Build the specified targets in the project. Specify each target separately, or use a semicolon or comma to separate multiple targets, as the following example shows:
/target:Resources;Compile

If you specify any targets by using this switch, they are run instead of any targets in the DefaultTargets attribute in the project file. For more information, see Target Build Order and How to: Specify Which Target to Build First.

A target is a group of tasks. For more information, see Targets.

properties.name: ToolsVersion
properties.help:
Specifies the version of the Toolset to use to build the project, as the following example shows: /toolsversion:3.5

By using this switch, you can build a project and specify a version that differs from the version that's specified in the Project Element (MSBuild). For more information, see Overriding ToolsVersion Settings.

For MSBuild 4.5, you can specify the following values for version: 2.0, 3.5, and 4.0. If you specify 4.0, the VisualStudioVersion build property specifies which sub-toolset to use. For more information, see the Sub-toolsets section of Toolset (ToolsVersion).

A Toolset consists of tasks, targets, and tools that are used to build an application. The tools include compilers such as csc.exe and vbc.exe. For more information about Toolsets, see Toolset (ToolsVersion), Standard and Custom Toolset Configurations, and Multitargeting. Note: The toolset version isn't the same as the target framework, which is the version of the .NET Framework on which a project is built to run. For more information, see Target Framework and Target Platform.

properties.name: MSBuildVersion
properties.help: Specifies the version of MSBuild to use.

properties.name: Verbosity
properties.help:
Specifies the amount of information to display in the build log. Each logger displays events based on the verbosity level that you set for that logger.

You can specify the following verbosity levels: q[uiet], m[inimal], n[ormal], d[etailed], and diag[nostic].

The following setting is an example: /verbosity:quiet

properties.name: MSBuildPlatform
properties.help: Specifies the MSBuild platform (e.g. x86, x64).

properties.name: Loggers
properties.help: Specifies the loggers to use to log events from MSBuild.

properties.name: NoConsoleLogger
properties.help: Disable the default console logger, and don't log events to the console.
